  • Technical and business skill development
  • Increased employability after graduation

The programmes are ideal for individuals who prefer practical learning combined with formal education.

What Apprentices Receive

Successful applicants can expect numerous professional and personal development opportunities.

Benefits include:

1. Paid Employment

All Google apprenticeships are paid positions.

Participants receive:

  • Competitive salary
  • Benefits
  • Professional development support
  • Employee learning resources

Google confirms that every apprenticeship is a paid opportunity.

2. Real Google Work Experience

Apprentices become active members of Google teams rather than simply observing workplace activities.

Depending on the programme, participants may contribute to:

  • Software development
  • Customer engineering
  • Digital marketing
  • UX and media design
  • Sales operations
  • Data centre operations
  • AI and machine learning projects
  • Cloud technologies
  • Communication campaigns

3. Structured External Training

Each apprenticeship combines work experience with formal education delivered by accredited training providers, universities, or vocational institutions.

Training may include:

  • Classroom learning
  • Technical bootcamps
  • University coursework
  • Vocational qualifications
  • Industry certifications

4. Professional Skill Development

Throughout the programme apprentices strengthen:

  • Communication
  • Leadership
  • Teamwork
  • Problem solving
  • Project management
  • Presentation skills
  • Technical expertise
  • Customer engagement

5. Career Coaching

Google provides career coaching throughout the apprenticeship.

Support includes:

  • Career planning
  • Resume development
  • Interview preparation
  • Professional networking
  • Guidance for future employment

Learn More Here

 

Countries Offering Google Apprenticeships

Google currently offers apprenticeship opportunities across several European countries.

Available locations include:

France

Available programmes include:

Sales and Marketing Apprenticeship

Duration:

  • 12 months

Participants:

  • Study at a university or engineering school
  • Complete apprenticeship training through a Centre de Formation d’Apprentis (CFA)
  • Work part-time at Google

Applications typically open during spring for autumn entry.

Software Engineering Apprenticeship

Duration:

  • 12–36 months

Focus areas include:

  • Computer Science
  • Software Engineering
  • Technical development

Participants combine university studies with practical software engineering experience.

Customer Engineering Apprenticeship

Duration:

  • 24 months

Study areas include:

  • Computer Science
  • Infrastructure
  • Application Modernization
  • Security
  • AI and Machine Learning
  • Data Science
  • Networking

Ireland

Software Engineering Apprenticeship

Duration:

  • 24 months

Programme includes:

  • Six months of external training
  • On-the-job experience at Google
  • Partnership with FIT

Google notes that hiring for the 2025 cohort has closed, with recruitment for the 2026 cohort beginning in February.

Netherlands

Data Center Technician Apprenticeship

Programme duration varies between:

  • 20–34 weeks

Participants remain enrolled in school while completing practical workplace learning at Google’s data centres.

Switzerland

Application Development Apprenticeship (EFZ)

Duration:

  • Four years

The programme begins with one year of external training before apprentices join Google teams.

Interactive Media Design Apprenticeship (EFZ)

Duration:

  • Four years

Participants divide their time between:

  • School (approximately 40%)
  • Workplace learning (approximately 60%)

United Kingdom

Google has previously offered:

Digital Business Apprenticeship

Duration:

  • 15 months

Delivered in partnership with Makers.

Although recruitment for the 2026 cohort has closed, similar opportunities may reopen in future cycles.

Software Engineering Apprenticeship

Duration:

  • 24 months

Includes:

  • Fourteen-week technical bootcamp
  • Workplace learning
  • Level 4 Software Developer qualification

Eligibility Requirements

Eligibility varies depending on the apprenticeship and country.

Generally, applicants should:

  • Meet the minimum qualifications listed for the programme
  • Be eligible to work in the country where the apprenticeship is located
  • Meet educational requirements
  • Submit all required application documents

Google notes that apprenticeships are generally available to individuals aged 18 and above, except in Switzerland where local requirements differ.

Can International Applicants Apply?

Yes.

International applicants may apply provided they:

  • Have legal authorization to work in the country
  • Meet programme requirements
  • Secure any necessary visas where applicable

Applicants are responsible for obtaining work authorization if required.

Can Graduates Apply?

Yes.

Google welcomes applicants who already possess university degrees, provided the degree is in a different subject from the apprenticeship they wish to pursue.

This makes apprenticeships an excellent pathway for career changers.

Application Documents

Applicants generally need to submit:

  • Updated CV or resume
  • Cover letter explaining motivation
  • Additional documents required for the specific apprenticeship

Applications that are incomplete will not be considered.

Selection Process

The recruitment process typically includes:

  1. Application review
  2. Recruiter screening
  3. Eligibility assessment
  4. Interviews or assessment centre
  5. Final selection

Each apprenticeship may have slightly different procedures.

Will Apprentices Receive Permanent Jobs?

Google explains that apprenticeships are designed to prepare participants for entry-level careers.

After completing the programme, apprentices may:

  • Apply for full-time roles at Google
  • Continue their education
  • Pursue careers elsewhere in the technology industry

Employment is not guaranteed but participants receive career coaching throughout the programme.

Relocation Support

Where eligible, Google may provide relocation assistance.

Details are discussed during recruitment and offer stages.

Support for Applicants with Disabilities

Google provides accommodations for candidates with disabilities throughout the recruitment process.

Accommodation requests are handled by Google’s dedicated accessibility teams.

Why Google Apprenticeships Stand Out

Google Apprenticeships combine several advantages rarely found together:

  • Paid employment
  • Professional training
  • World-class work experience
  • Academic study
  • Career coaching
  • Networking opportunities
  • Exposure to cutting-edge technologies
  • Pathways into technology careers

For individuals looking to begin or transition into careers in software engineering, AI, cloud computing, marketing, customer engineering, UX, or digital business, these programmes provide an exceptional opportunity to build practical experience while continuing formal education.

Application Timeline

Application dates vary depending on:

  • Country
  • Programme
  • Training partner

Many programmes recruit seasonally, with applications opening in spring or summer for autumn starts, while some programmes have specific annual recruitment windows. Applicants are encouraged to regularly monitor the Google Apprenticeships portal for new openings.

Learn More Here

How to Apply

Interested applicants should:

  1. Visit Google’s Apprenticeships Portal.
  2. Browse available programmes by country and profession.
  3. Review the eligibility requirements for their chosen apprenticeship.
  4. Prepare an updated CV/resume and tailored cover letter.
  5. Submit all required application materials before the relevant deadline.

Because application windows differ across programmes, candidates should check frequently for newly opened roles.

Conclusion

Google Apprenticeships 2026–2027 represent one of the most attractive work-and-study opportunities available for aspiring professionals seeking practical experience at a global technology company. By combining paid employment, structured learning, mentorship, and career development, these programmes equip participants with the technical and professional skills needed to succeed in today’s competitive job market.

Whether your interests lie in software engineering, AI, customer engineering, marketing, UX design, digital business, or data centre operations, a Google Apprenticeship can serve as a powerful stepping stone toward a rewarding career in technology and innovation. Prospective applicants are encouraged to explore available programmes, prepare strong application materials, and monitor recruitment timelines for their preferred country and discipline.

Discover more Global Internship opportunities on Opportunities for Youth (OFY) HERE.

The post Google Apprenticeships 2026–2027: Paid Work-and-Study Opportunities at Google Across Europe | Launch Your Career in Software Engineering, AI, Marketing, UX, Customer Engineering & More appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
